mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations. Higher FEDFUNDS reduces present value of future corporate cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. This decline in aggregate demand produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points in 2 months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between UNRATE changes and subsequent 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1.3 million units signals contraction in residential construction demand and forward-looking weakness in consumer wealth. This demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity markets price in reduced economic activity.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of instances when HOUST < 1300, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and subsequent 20-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness and reduced manufacturing capacity utilization. This supply contraction signals earnings pressure on industrial equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 52% of triggered instances, or rolling 3-month correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day returns is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both decline simultaneously, indicating synchronized demand destruction across consumer and residential sectors. This joint demand shock produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity markets reprice growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.1% AND HOUST < 1300 thousand
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380230.0 million and HOUST at 1331.0 thousand as of 2007-09-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances when both conditions are triggered, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) has Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 50,000 persons per month while unemployment rate rises simultaneously, signaling labor market deterioration. This joint labor market weakness produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 50 thousand AND UNRATE > 4.6%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138265.0 thousand and UNRATE at 4.7% as of 2007-09-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between PAYEMS and UNRATE changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%), inflation measured by CPIAUCSL growth above 2.5% year-over-year signals stagflationary pressure. Central banks maintain restrictive policy despite growth concerns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates remain elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.94% and CPIAUCSL at 207.738 as of 2007-09-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of stagflationary tightening instances, or the interaction between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL growth does not significantly predict forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle economic slowdown. In this regime, equity valuations compress as terminal growth rates are revised downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism is regime-conditional: it activates only when growth falls below potential.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11520.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2007-04-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 2%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.
